action.reasoning: Observation: FP-MLP baseline stable at ~0.163 test AP (val AP reached 0.1776), loss decreases cleanly to ~0.20 without NaNs, caching works, predict.py succeeds. Earlier PyG segfaults (exit 139 on nn/data imports) blocked GNNs; current best from iter7. Analysis: MLP on 2048-bit Morgan FP is limited vs graph structure (task provides node/edge feats for GNN); val AP still improving at epoch 15 so longer training + BN+lower dropout can help but prior BN caused NaNs (needs grad_clip + masked loss). Root cause of past AP crashes was unmasked NaNs in BCE/sklearn. No jsonl metrics written yet. Decision: First test if PyG imports work now (post many runs, env may have stabilized); if yes, switch to GINEConv GNN (local Atom/Bond encoders, no ogb) for stronger baseline on provided graphs (expect >0.20 AP); update all 3 files incrementally, use DataLoader with Batch, mask NaNs in loss, log to metrics.jsonl per epoch, train 8 epochs (time-safe ~15min), save best.pt, submit_eval immediately for new baseline + time measurement before longer runs.
action.command:
python -c '
import torch
import torch_geometric
print("torch:", torch.__version__)
print("pyg imported successfully")
from torch_geometric.nn import GINEConv, global_mean_pool, BatchNorm
from torch_geometric.data import Data, Batch
from torch_geometric.loader import DataLoader
print("All PyG components imported OK")
import os
print("Data dir:", os.environ.get("RAB_DATA_DIR", "/data"))
print("Test successful")
' && echo 'Import test passed, proceeding to train' && python train.py
